name: Adobe Analytics
notes: Adobe Analytics (formerly SiteCatalyst). Visit ID cookies (s_vi, s_ecid) are persistent identifiers shared across the Adobe Marketing Cloud. Frequently invisible in CCPA opt-out implementations because the consent module is configured separately from CMP. Pre-consent firing surfaces in pixel-as-sale enforcement (Sephora theory extends here).

name: Adobe Target
notes: Adobe Target (A/B testing and personalization). The mbox cookie persists user assignment to test groups; profile data is uploaded to Adobe Marketing Cloud and joined with Analytics + AAM. Sharing audiences via Adobe is functionally a CCPA sale under the Sephora pixel-as-sale theory.

name: Adobe Audience Manager
notes: Adobe Audience Manager (DMP). The demdex.net domain is Adobe's universal user-sync endpoint — a single ID joins Analytics, Target, and ad-platform audience exports. Direct CCPA sale; broad sharing of identifiers with third-party DSPs. Pre-consent firing is a high-exposure violation.

name: Hotjar
notes: Hotjar session replay, heatmaps, surveys. Records mouse movement, clicks, scroll, form input. The session replay product is a textbook CIPA §631 wiretap surface — multiple class actions in 2024-2025 named Hotjar specifically. Pre-consent firing is the canonical CIPA fact pattern.

name: Microsoft Clarity
notes: Microsoft Clarity session replay (free Hotjar alternative — widely adopted because of zero cost). Same CIPA §631 wiretap exposure as Hotjar. Microsoft surfaces the data to Bing Ads automatically if Bing Ads is also installed, which doubles the exposure to CCPA sale claims.

name: Mixpanel
notes: Mixpanel product analytics. Tracks every user event with persistent distinct_id. Identify() calls send PII (email, name, phone) to Mixpanel for behavioral profiling. CCPA covers if PII is included. CIPA exposure when behavioral events are recorded pre-consent.

name: Heap Analytics
notes: Heap Analytics. Auto-captures every click, page view, form interaction. Same risk profile as Mixpanel. The autocapture mode pulls more data than most CMP configs anticipate — privacy review often missed during deployment.

name: Amplitude
notes: Amplitude product analytics. Similar exposure profile to Mixpanel and Heap. Pre-consent firing of user_id-tied events is a CCPA disclosure issue, especially for product-led growth (PLG) SaaS with email-as-identifier.

name: Segment
notes: Segment Customer Data Platform (now Twilio). Forwards event data to dozens of downstream destinations including Meta, Google, LinkedIn. A single Segment install is functionally an aggregator of every CCPA-sale risk on the site. Disclosure should explicitly enumerate destinations enabled in Segment's pipeline.

name: HubSpot
notes: HubSpot CRM + Marketing Hub tracking. hubspotutk is the persistent visitor identifier joined to the contact record on form submission, enabling cross-session marketing automation. Pre-consent firing in EU/Quebec is a clear GDPR/Law 25 violation; CCPA disclosure required for the visitor-to-contact join.

name: Marketo
notes: Marketo Engage (Adobe-owned, B2B marketing automation). _mkto_trk joins anonymous visits to known contacts post-form-submission. Same risk profile as HubSpot but typically in regulated industries (financial services, healthcare) where the joining surfaces additional sector-specific exposure.

name: Pardot
notes: Salesforce Pardot (B2B marketing automation, now Marketing Cloud Account Engagement). Visitor tracking and form-to-contact join. Same exposure profile as Marketo. Often installed alongside Salesforce CRM which extends the data trail to the company's CRM database.

name: Klaviyo
notes: Klaviyo email + SMS marketing (Shopify-heavy ecosystem). __kla_id is a persistent visitor identifier joined to a subscriber record on email capture or purchase, enabling cross-session and cross-device targeting. Pre-consent firing is a CCPA disclosure issue and an EU GDPR violation.

name: Drift
notes: Drift conversational marketing / chatbot. Records visitor and (when identified) email-to-conversation linkage. CIPA exposure for transcript recording without consent — the chat widget records the human-side conversation, which class actions in 2024 framed as wiretapping.

name: Intercom
notes: Intercom messaging platform. Same CIPA exposure profile as Drift — chat transcript recording without consent is the named claim in multiple 2024-2025 class actions. Cookie classification typically C0003 (functional) but the recording itself triggers wiretap liability.

name: Zendesk
notes: Zendesk customer support. Session cookies for the help center and authenticated portal. Lower exposure than Drift/Intercom (no chat recording in the base product), but Zendesk Chat + Sunshine Conversations add the same CIPA risk surface.

name: LiveRamp
notes: LiveRamp identity resolution (universal-ID provider). Joins email-based identifiers to a cross-site rampID, enabling cookieless tracking across publisher networks. Functionally a CCPA sale — the entire product is identity sharing with the ad ecosystem. Pre-consent firing is direct exposure.

name: LiveIntent
notes: LiveIntent identity resolution (email-based universal ID). Same exposure profile as LiveRamp. The product hashes email addresses, then joins to a cross-site identifier for ad targeting. Disclosure must explicitly name the email-hash-as-ID mechanism for CCPA compliance.

name: Qualtrics SiteIntercept
notes: Qualtrics SiteIntercept (XM Survey targeting). Reads session history + URL referrer + visit count to fingerprint and target survey prompts. Classified as behavioral targeting by CPPA when used to inform marketing decisions. Pre-consent firing surfaces in CCPA opt-out enforcement (see Tractor Supply 2025).

name: YouTube (embed)
notes: YouTube video embeds set 3P advertising cookies on iframe load. Classic CIPA wiretap claim surface — the embedded player communicates viewing history to Google before consent. Use youtube-nocookie.com (privacy-enhanced mode) to suppress. Pre-consent embed = CCPA sale (Disney CA AG 2026 precedent extends here).

name: Facebook (page-set cookies)
notes: Facebook/Meta cookies set when the page loads connect.facebook.net (Pixel, Login-with-Facebook button, social plugins). ps_n is a fingerprinting cookie set by facebook.com during pixel calls. Same pixel-as-sale theory as the main Meta Pixel finding — Sephora $1.2M, ongoing CIPA class actions.

name: Google (general)
notes: Google ecosystem cookies set when the page calls google.com (Google Fonts, reCAPTCHA, embedded Google Maps, Google Tag Manager script load). NID is Google's preference cookie that doubles as a cross-site identifier; CONSENT carries Consent Mode signals. Borderline-targeting category; surfaces in CPPA enforcement when not disclosed in vendor list.

name: Cloudflare (bot management)
notes: Cloudflare bot management cookie. Set by the Cloudflare edge to distinguish bots from humans for the next 30 minutes. Classified strictly essential (security/anti-fraud); does NOT require consent under GDPR/CCPA/Law 25. Listed here so the scanner classifies it as essential rather than flagging as unknown.

name: NextRoll
notes: Named in National Casual Dining Chain lawsuit (Jan 2026). S3 confirmed firing despite opt-out.

name: The Trade Desk
notes: Named in National Casual Dining Chain lawsuit. S3 confirmed firing despite opt-out.

name: LiveRamp
notes: Named in National Casual Dining Chain lawsuit. Suspected to operate via network requests — requires HAR analysis.

name: PubMatic
notes: Named in National Casual Dining Chain lawsuit. Suspected to operate via network requests — requires HAR analysis.

name: Meta
notes: _fbp = Facebook Pixel base cookie. CNIL fined Meta 150M EUR (2025).

name: Google / DoubleClick
notes: IDE = DoubleClick targeting cookie. CNIL fined Google 325M EUR (2025).

name: Microsoft / Bing
notes: Universal Event Tracking (UET) cookies + Clarity session recording cookies.

name: Pinterest
notes: Pinterest Tag conversion tracking.

name: Google Analytics
notes: _ga = GA4 client ID. Under Advanced Consent Mode, cookieless pings still fire in G100 state.

name: TikTok
notes: TikTok Pixel and Events API tracking cookies. Named in multiple CIPA complaints.

name: LinkedIn
notes: LinkedIn Insight Tag and CAPI cookies.

name: Snapchat
notes: Snap Pixel conversion tracking.

name: Twitter/X
notes: Twitter/X conversion tracking pixel.

name: Reddit
notes: Reddit conversion pixel and attribution.

name: Criteo
notes: Criteo retargeting. Heavy in DTC ecommerce.

name: Klaviyo
notes: Klaviyo email/SMS marketing platform. Common in Shopify DTC.

name: Hotjar
notes: Session recording. CIPA wiretap theory applies to session replay tools.

name: FullStory
notes: Session recording. CIPA wiretap theory applies.

name: ContentSquare
notes: Session recording and experience analytics. CIPA wiretap theory.

name: Attentive
notes: SMS/email marketing. Common in DTC ecommerce.

name: Optimizely
notes: A/B testing and experimentation platform.

name: Amplitude
notes: Product analytics.

name: Mixpanel
notes: Product analytics.

name: Segment
notes: Customer data platform. Routes data to downstream vendors.

name: LiveIntent
notes: Identity resolution for email-based advertising.

name: Adform
notes: European DSP. Common on DTC brands with EU ad spend.

name: Amazon Ads
notes: Amazon advertising pixel and attribution.

name: ABTasty
notes: A/B testing and personalization.

name: Taboola
notes: Native advertising / content recommendation.

name: Outbrain
notes: Native advertising / content recommendation.

name: Yotpo
notes: Reviews and UGC platform. Common on Shopify stores.

name: Trustpilot
notes: Review platform widget.

name: Exponea/Bloomreach
notes: CDP and marketing automation. Common in ecommerce.

name: Lotame
notes: Data management platform / audience enrichment.

name: Claritas
notes: Audience segmentation and identity resolution.

name: Bazaarvoice
notes: Ratings and reviews platform.

name: Microsoft Clarity
notes: Microsoft session-replay + heatmap product. Session-replay tools are central to active CIPA / state-wiretap class actions (Javier v. Pomerantz line). Records mouse movement, clicks, scrolls, form interaction.

name: Hotjar
notes: Session replay + heatmaps + form analytics. Named in multiple CIPA filings. Records keystrokes by default.

name: FullStory
notes: Session-replay platform. Heavy CIPA exposure — Williams v. DDR Media named FullStory specifically.

name: Mouseflow
notes: Session replay. CIPA exposure equivalent to Hotjar/FullStory.

name: Smartlook
notes: Session replay. Named in 2025 California class-action filings.

name: Quantum Metric
notes: Enterprise session-replay platform. Used heavily by financial services + retail.

name: Glassbox
notes: Session replay + digital experience monitoring. Specifically cited in Calvert v. Red Robin (Sept 2024).

name: Heap
notes: Auto-capture product analytics — instruments every click/form/page without explicit tagging. Privacy concern: captures more than the implementer realizes.

name: Pendo
notes: Product analytics + in-app guides. Tracks behavior + identifies users via embedded SDK.

name: Crazy Egg
notes: Heatmaps + session recording.

name: Lucky Orange
notes: Session replay + heatmaps. Named in California CIPA filings.

name: Inspectlet
notes: Session-replay tool — first defendant in Yale v. Clicktale-line CIPA class actions.

name: Drift
notes: Conversational marketing / chat widget. CIPA exposure when chat logs are captured without consent banner.

name: Intercom
notes: Chat widget + product analytics. Captures conversation transcripts + user identifiers.

name: LiveChat
notes: Chat widget. CIPA exposure when chat content is logged without consent.

name: Zendesk
notes: Zendesk Chat (formerly Zopim) + support widgets. Same CIPA exposure as other chat tools.

name: Tawk.to
notes: Free chat widget — extremely common on SMB and lead-gen sites.

name: Adobe Analytics
notes: Adobe Experience Cloud analytics (formerly SiteCatalyst). AMCV cookies carry the Marketing Cloud Visitor ID — cross-property identifier.

name: Adobe Target
notes: Adobe Target — A/B testing + personalization. Reads visitor ID for segment membership.

name: Adobe Audience Manager
notes: Adobe DMP — cross-site identity resolution for advertising audiences. High CCPA/GDPR exposure.

name: Marketo
notes: Adobe Marketo Engage — B2B marketing automation. _mkto_trk tracks anonymous visitors across the funnel.

name: HubSpot
notes: HubSpot CRM + Marketing Hub. hubspotutk is the persistent visitor identifier — joins form submissions to the CRM contact record.

name: Salesforce Pardot
notes: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot). B2B lead tracking.

name: Mailchimp
notes: Mailchimp email + popup widgets. Lower CIPA risk than chat tools — surface is email subscription, not full chat capture.

name: Klaviyo
notes: DTC ecommerce marketing platform. Tracks anonymous visitors via __kla_id and merges with email identity post-signup.

name: ActiveCampaign
notes: Marketing automation + CRM. Site-tracking cookie ties anonymous behavior to known contacts.

name: Tealium iQ
notes: Enterprise tag-management system. utag_main carries Tealium's anonymous visitor identifier + session state across the funnel. Foundational risk: misconfigured TMS can defeat the CMP.

name: Segment
notes: Twilio Segment CDP — server-side identity routing. ajs_anonymous_id flows downstream to whatever destinations are wired in.

name: mParticle
notes: Enterprise CDP — competitor to Segment. Same downstream-routing concern.

name: Snowplow
notes: Open-source enterprise behavioral analytics. Often self-hosted; cookie names match the standard schema.

name: Quora
notes: Quora Pixel — B2B / consumer ad conversion tracking on Quora's network.

name: Bing Ads
notes: Microsoft Universal Event Tracking (UET) — Bing Ads conversion + remarketing pixel. Common second ad-network behind Google Ads.

name: Yandex Metrika
notes: Russian analytics platform — equivalent of Google Analytics. Sanctions / cross-border data-flow exposure for US companies still running it post-2022.

name: Baidu Analytics
notes: Baidu Tongji analytics — common on China-focused sites.

name: AppLovin
notes: Mobile + CTV ad network. High legal exposure following recent FTC + DOJ scrutiny of CTV data flows.

name: RTB House
notes: Programmatic display + personalized retargeting. EU-headquartered; GDPR-exposed.

name: ID5
notes: Universal cookieless ID — one of the post-3rd-party-cookie identity solutions. Cross-publisher resolution: high regulatory scrutiny.

name: LiveIntent (Audience)
notes: Email-based identity resolution + advertising. High CCPA exposure — ties anonymous behavior to email identities.

name: Cloudflare Web Analytics
notes: Cookieless privacy-focused analytics (no persistent identifier). Flagged here so the scanner doesn't false-positive it as a tracking cookie.

name: Plausible Analytics
notes: Cookieless EU-hosted analytics. Same false-positive guard as Cloudflare Insights.

name: Fathom Analytics
notes: Cookieless privacy-focused analytics. False-positive guard.
